name: Budget trim — survives vs dropped
description: Verifies that retrieval scoring distinguishes 'candidate selection found the right note' from 'candidate found it but budget trim dropped it'. Uses maxInputTokens=400 to deterministically force the viewer_lore→5 trim step regardless of how much realistic content we seed. Note insertion order matters: SQL returns notes ORDER BY last_confirmed_at DESC, ties broken by rowid DESC, so the LAST-inserted notes come back first. The runner inserts in fixture order, so the final 5 notes in this list will survive trim.

boxedlikefish.fact: Used to mod for a different streamer back in 2022 before joining this community
Was the first person to use the cheese raid emote in chat
Did a guest appearance once during a collab night, was very funny
Made the 'git gud' motivational poster the streamer keeps in the corner of OBS
Started learning Japanese specifically to play FromSoft games in original audio
Adopted a black cat last weekend named Friede after the boss
Just got a new mechanical keyboard and types louder than usual on stream
Has been working on a fan theory connecting Dark Souls and Bloodborne
Mentioned this morning that she finally beat the Dancer of the Boreal Valley after 47 attempts
Just said in chat that she's about to start a new playthrough

1.reason: boxedlikefish mentions the bot directly — production retrieval fetches lore for the message speaker, so boxedlikefish's notes come back. The budget cap (200 tokens) forces aggressive viewer-lore trim. SQL returns notes by rowid DESC (insertion order reversed) so 'boxedlikefish-most-recent' is in position 0 — it survives. 'boxedlikefish-old-mod' was inserted FIRST in the fixture, so it's last in SQL DESC order — it gets dropped. Recall=0.5 confirms the trim worked. Recall=1.0 means the budget didn't trim (cap is too loose). Recall=0.0 means SQL retrieval isn't pulling boxedlikefish's notes at all.
